name: Calar Alto Loop from Purchena
name_original: Subida al Collado del Ramal y al Castillo de Sierro — circular desde Purchena
description: This challenging 42.7-mile road cycling loop from Purchena climbs 5630 feet to the Calar Alto Observatory, offering spectacular views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Purchena are set within the diverse landscapes of the Almanzora Valley, nestled at the foot of the Sierra de los Filabres. The region features a mix of forests ascending into mountains and historical routes, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Routes range from gentle slopes along old railway lines to strenuous mountain passes with considerable elevation gains. This environment provides options for both leisurely rides and challenging climbs through its natural features. Best road cycling routes around Purchena The most popular road cycling route is Climb to Collado del Ramal and Castillo de Sierro - circular from Purchena, a 42.7 miles (68.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Velefique climb – Climb to Alto de Velefique loop from Sierro, a difficult 50.7 miles (81.6 km) path. This route includes substantial climbing, reaching high points in the Sierra de los Filabres. Local road cyclists also love the Almanzora – Iron Bridge of Cantoria loop from Estación de Fines-Olula, a 29.9 miles (48.1 km) trail leading through the Almanzora Valley, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Road cycling around Purchena is defined by the Sierra de los Filabres, the Almanzora Valley, and the Vía Verde del Almanzora. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Purchena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Purchena?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Purchena, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the Almanzora Valley and the Sierra de los Filabres, offering everything from gentle rides to challenging mountain climbs.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Purchena?
A: Yes, Purchena offers several challenging routes with significant elevation gains, particularly in the Sierra de los Filabres. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987347805/climb-to-collado-del-ramal-and-castillo-de-sierro-circular-from-purchena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Collado del Ramal and Castillo de Sierro - circular from Purchena&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 42.7 miles (68.7 km) with substantial climbing. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31816317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velefique climb – Climb to Alto de Velefique loop from Sierro&lt;/a&gt;, known for its strenuous ascent to high points in the Sierra de los Filabres.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling routes in Purchena?
A: For easier rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almanzora – Iron Bridge of Cantoria loop from Estación de Fines-Olula&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 29.9 miles (48.1 km) path through the Almanzora Valley. The Vía Verde del Almanzora is also an excellent option for a more relaxed experience, following an old railway line with panoramic views and accessible terrain, suitable for families and those seeking less demanding cycling.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on road cycling routes around Purchena?
A: Road cycling around Purchena features highly varied terrain. You'll find routes along the flat, scenic Almanzora Valley, including sections of the Vía Verde del Almanzora. However, many routes ascend into the Sierra de los Filabres, offering significant climbs and descents, with some routes featuring over 2,000 meters of elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31816317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velefique climb&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning vistas. The Silveria Viewpoint, located at approximately 1,000 meters above sea level, provides magnificent natural views. Cyclists can also encounter historical elements like ancient viaducts and tunnels along the Vía Verde del Almanzora. Other notable landmarks include the historical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5037637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct near Purchena&lt;/a&gt; and various old railway stations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4807759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serón Railway Station&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5306706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Tíjola train Station&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the region's railway heritage.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Purchena are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987347805/climb-to-collado-del-ramal-and-castillo-de-sierro-circular-from-purchena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Collado del Ramal and Castillo de Sierro - circular from Purchena&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a comprehensive tour of the area's mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Purchena?
A: Purchena's climate makes it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides and climbing. Summers can be hot, especially in the valley, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during these months. Winters are mild, making it possible to cycle, though higher mountain passes might be cooler.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Purchena?
A: Yes, Purchena, as a town, typically offers public parking facilities. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the starting points, but generally, you can find parking in or near the town center or at designated trailheads for the Vía Verde del Almanzora.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the routes themselves, the region boasts several natural attractions. You can find gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Estrecho de Urrácal&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2649812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cerrada de Valentín (La Cerrá), Tíjola&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2650515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Huevo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4625289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Piedra Lobera&lt;/a&gt; also offers a beautiful natural viewpoint.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: While specific cafes directly on every route may vary, Purchena and the surrounding towns in the Almanzora Valley offer various establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments. It's a good idea to plan your route to pass through these towns or carry sufficient supplies, especially on longer or more remote mountain sections.

Q: What is the Vía Verde del Almanzora and is it suitable for road bikes?
A: The Vía Verde del Almanzora is a 'Green Way' that follows an old railway line, providing a generally flat and accessible path. It's suitable for road bikes, especially those with slightly wider tires, offering a smooth ride with panoramic views of the valley and historical railway structures. It's an excellent option for leisurely rides and enjoying the scenery.
tips: Ascent from Serón to Ramal hill passing in front of Las Menas and that will make us warm our legs.
The road comes from the Ramal pass and you can see the center of Bacares and the small valley in which it is located. In the background, you can also see the castle of Bacares.

Medieval castle-fortress of Islamic Berber style. With a difference of 300 m above the river that crosses the town of Sierro, this castle stands out for its prominence and good views of the entire territory that surrounds it. It dates from the 12th century and its reconstruction is from the year 2011.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Calar Alto Loop from Purchena?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this challenging route is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally more stable and temperatures are milder, making the long ascent more manageable. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter can bring cold temperatures and even snow at the summit of Calar Alto.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Purchena?
faqs.answer: Purchena serves as the natural starting and ending point for this loop. You should be able to find suitable parking within the town of Purchena itself, allowing for convenient access to the route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Calar Alto Loop?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly on paved roads, but it features a long and arduous ascent to Calar Alto, with sustained gradients often around 7%. The terrain transitions from forested areas at lower elevations to a more barren, high-mountain environment. The descent offers fast stretches on quiet roads.

faqs.question: Is this a suitable route for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is considered 'difficult' and demands a very good level of fitness. The continuous climb to over 2,000 meters and the significant elevation gain make it a substantial endurance test. It is best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Sierra de los Filabres?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or generally within the Sierra de los Filabres. You can enjoy the natural beauty and challenging climbs freely.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable highlights. You'll experience the challenging Ascent to Collado del Ramal from Serón, enjoy panoramic Views of Bacares, and pass by the historic Sierro Castle. A major highlight is reaching the Calar Alto astronomical observatory at the summit, with its unique white domes.

faqs.question: What makes the Calar Alto climb so special for cyclists?
faqs.answer: The Calar Alto climb is renowned for its challenging nature, often compared to the 'Alpe d'Huez' of Spain due to its length and switchbacks. It has been featured multiple times in the Vuelta a España, making it a bucket-list climb for many. Reaching the surreal Calar Alto Observatory at 2,168 meters provides a massive sense of achievement and incredible panoramic views.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this ride, especially considering the altitude?
faqs.answer: Given the significant elevation gain and high altitude, be prepared for varying temperatures. Even on a hot day, it can be considerably cooler at the summit. It's advisable to bring layers, including a windproof jacket and gloves, especially for the descent. Always carry sufficient water, nutrition, and essential bike repair tools.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the Calar Alto Loop?
faqs.answer: While Purchena offers amenities, options for cafes or refueling points become very limited, if not non-existent, once you begin the main ascent towards Calar Alto. It's crucial to carry enough food and water for the entire ride, especially for the long climb and descent.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a demanding road cycling route designed for experienced cyclists, not a leisurely family outing. Therefore, it is not suitable for families with young children on bikes. Similarly, due to the nature of road cycling and the challenging terrain, it is not considered a dog-friendly activity for pets to accompany you on the ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant portions of the [CIMA AM08] Calar Alto * Tíjola and [CIMA AM08] Calar Alto * Serón - Las Menas routes. Smaller sections also overlap with the Transandalus, Las Yeseras de Suflí, and Vereda Bayarque trails.

waypoints.name: Ascent to Collado del Ramal from Serón
